tax: True Elementals is the phylum of pure single-element life beneath the Elementals kingdom. Membership requires a body composed wholly of one primary element and a stable identity within one of the four classes — Earth, Fire, Water, or Air. Composition and formation mechanics fix the placement, never colour, size, or danger. A new class would require a primary element not already represented; the four observed classes sit directly beneath this phylum.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a mass of one element — stone, flame, water, ice, or air — behaving against the ordinary logic of its medium. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a medium-specific sign left where the body stood: tremor or shed mineral, scorch or ash, a high-water mark or breath-frost, spiralling dust or a guttering flame — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: True Elementals occupies regions of concentrated elemental pressure throughout the Throat: faulted stone, vents, sumps and falls, cold seeps, and draught and storm shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where concentrated earth, fire, water, or air pressure animates its medium into a coherent body and recur with the same pressure renews the body from the same medium after it disperses, sustaining themselves on tectonic, combustive, hydraulic, or atmospheric pressure depending on class.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members are territorial expressions of their medium, lying inert until disturbed and answering as landslide, wildfire, flood, or storm; their decisions are pressure thresholds, not plans. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a pure-element body, immunity or resistance to the mundane hazards of its own element, and a defining weakness to disruption by an opposed force. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each class and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: elemental motion, engulfing, burning, drowning, freezing, buffeting, shocking, or stone-shaping, each bounded to the body's own element. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, True Elementals matters because they account for the gorge's most dangerous 'natural' events — rockfalls, fires, floods, and storms that move with intent. It lets survey reports sort elemental hazards by element, so a crew knows whether the threat is stone, fire, water, or air before it is even seen without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: True Elementals is the phylum-rank grouping of organisms built wholly from one of the four primary elements within the True Elementals. Its members share a body that is pure earth, fire, water, or air — no flesh, organs, or animal ancestry — formed from concentrated elemental pressure rather than by breeding, and bound to the medium that sustains it, and divide below into class groups by the primary element of the body: earth, fire, water, or air. They explain why parts of the Throat behave like a plane intruding into a cavern: a walking bench, a flame that hunts, a sump that rears, a shaft that throws.
